scope: FQ-00 v0.6.1 closeout repair: legacy-migration duplication fix, smoke coverage, v0.6 packet metadata, EOF whitespace, interrupted-session file repair.
summary: Legacy pre-v0.6 characters migrating an old world save's player inventory now have items_granted marked during migration, so a later _grant_role_items() is a no-op instead of stacking role starter items onto the migrated inventory; brand-new legacy characters with no old world data still receive their starter grant. Two smoke checks (fq00_legacy_migration_marks_items_granted, fq00_no_duplicate_role_items_after_legacy_migration) run the full load path plus a forced regrant; suite 122 -> 124, PASS. The v0.6 Atlas/BOH packets now record real closeout commit 828aae4 (was a placeholder) and the Atlas packet's profiles match the public repo manifest. The working tree also contained NUL-padded truncated tails in game_root.gd, smoke_test.gd, and both v0.6 packets from an interrupted prior session; tails were restored from 828aae4 with the FQ-00 additions preserved and a repo-wide NUL scan verified clean.
acceptance: Old-format world inventory migrates once (dirt count matches old save exactly after migration)
Role starter items do not duplicate during migration (regrant no-op asserted)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 windowed Godot run -> smoke_results.json PASS 124/124 at 2026-07-04T11:58:43
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 124/124 PASS including both fq00_* checks
decisions: migrated_from_world gates the items_granted mark: only characters that actually adopted a legacy world inventory skip the starter grant; empty-legacy characters remain brand new
FQ-00 evidence ships as a second, evidence-only commit so its packets can record the repair commit's real hash instead of a placeholder — the placeholder pattern is what FQ-00 existed to fix
Interrupted-session truncation repair was folded into FQ-00 rather than left as untracked corruption
risks: Smoke still mutates the real user://shell.json; interrupted runs can leave test characters
FQ-00 smoke block leaves the migrated test inventory on the player until Wave C re-injects a known inventory; safe with current test order
next_action: FQ-01: player health, damage, healing, and death loop.
